authors_text: Chen Gong,Zhou Yang,Yunpeng Bai,Junda He,Jieke Shi,Kecen Li,Arunesh Sinha,Bowen Xu,Xinwen Hou,David Lo,Tianhao Wang
title: BAFFLE: Hiding Backdoors in Offline Reinforcement Learning Datasets
title_zh: BAFFLE：在离线强化学习数据集中隐藏后门
abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) makes an agent learn from trial-and-error experiences gathered during the interaction with the environment. Recently, offline RL has become a popular RL paradigm because it saves the interactions with environments. In offline RL, data providers share large pre-collected datasets, and others can train high-quality agents without interacting with the environments. This paradigm has demonstrated effectiveness in critical tasks like robot control, autonomous driving, etc. However, less attention is paid to investigating the security threats to the offline RL system. This paper focuses on backdoor attacks, where some perturbations are added to the data (observations) such that given normal observations, the agent takes high-rewards actions, and low-reward actions on observations injected with triggers. In this paper, we propose Baffle (Backdoor Attack for Offline Reinforcement Learning), an approach that automatically implants backdoors to RL agents by poisoning the offline RL dataset, and evaluate how different offline RL algorithms react to this attack. Our experiments conducted on four tasks and four offline RL algorithms expose a disquieting fact: none of the existing offline RL algorithms is immune to such a backdoor attack. More specifically, Baffle modifies 10\% of the datasets for four tasks (3 robotic controls and 1 autonomous driving). Agents trained on the poisoned datasets perform well in normal settings. However, when triggers are presented, the agents' performance decreases drastically by 63.2\%, 53.9\%, 64.7\%, and 47.4\% in the four tasks on average. The backdoor still persists after fine-tuning poisoned agents on clean datasets. We further show that the inserted backdoor is also hard to be detected by a popular defensive method. This paper calls attention to developing more effective protection for the open-source offline RL dataset.
summary: Reinforcement learning (RL) makes an agent learn from trial-and-error experiences gathered during the interaction with the environment. Recently, offline RL has become a popular RL paradigm because it saves the interactions with environments. In offline RL, data providers share large pre-collected datasets, and others can train high-quality agents without interacting with the environments. This paradigm has demonstrated effectiveness in critical tasks like robot control, autonomous driving, etc. However, less attention is paid to investigating the security threats to the offline RL system. This paper focuses on backdoor attacks, where some perturbations are added to the data (observations) such that given normal observations, the agent takes high-rewards actions, and low-reward actions on observations injected with triggers. In this paper, we propose Baffle (Backdoor Attack for Offline Reinforcement Learning), an approach that automatically implants backdoors to RL agents by poisoning the offline RL dataset, and evaluate how different offline RL algorithms react to this attack. Our experiments conducted on four tasks and four offline RL algorithms expose a disquieting fact: none of the existing offline RL algorithms is immune to such a backdoor attack. More specifically, Baffle modifies 10\% of the datasets for four tasks (3 robotic controls and 1 autonomous driving). Agents trained on the poisoned datasets perform well in normal settings. However, when triggers are presented, the agents' performance decreases drastically by 63.2\%, 53.9\%, 64.7\%, and 47.4\% in the four tasks on average. The backdoor still persists after fine-tuning poisoned agents on clean datasets. We further show that the inserted backdoor is also hard to be detected by a popular defensive method. This paper calls attention to developing more effective protection for the open-source offline RL dataset.
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）使智能体从与环境交互过程中收集的试错经验中学习。最近，离线强化学习已经成为一种流行的强化学习范式，因为它节省了与环境的交互。在离线强化学习中，数据提供者共享大量预先收集的数据集，其他人可以在不与环境交互的情况下训练高质量的代理。这种范式在机器人控制、自动驾驶等关键任务中证明了有效性。然而，很少有人关注离线强化学习系统的安全威胁的研究。本文重点关注后门攻击，其中一些扰动被添加到数据（观察）中，以便在给定正常观察的情况下，代理对注入触发器的观察采取高奖励行动，并采取低奖励行动。在本文中，我们提出了 Baffle（离线强化学习的后门攻击），这是一种通过毒害离线 RL 数据集自动向 RL 代理植入后门的方法，并评估不同的离线 RL 算法对此攻击的反应。我们对四个任务和四个离线强化学习算法进行的实验揭示了一个令人不安的事实：现有的离线强化学习算法没有一个能够免受这种后门攻击的影响。更具体地说，Baffle 修改了 4 个任务（3 个机器人控制和 1 个自动驾驶）的 10% 的数据集。在中毒数据集上训练的智能体在正常设置下表现良好。然而，当出现触发器时，代理在这四个任务中的性能平均急剧下降 63.2%、53.9%、64.7% 和 47.4%。在干净的数据集上微调中毒代理后，后门仍然存在。我们进一步表明，插入的后门也很难被流行的防御方法检测到。本文呼吁关注为开源离线强化学习数据集开发更有效的保护。

AI.tldr: 本文提出BAFFLE方法，在离线强化学习数据集中植入后门，使智能体在正常情况下表现正常，但遇到特定触发器时性能显著下降。
AI.motivation: 离线强化学习因无需环境交互而广泛应用，但现有研究忽视了其数据集面临的安全威胁，尤其是后门攻击风险。
AI.method: 通过修改离线数据集10%的样本植入后门，使智能体在正常观测下执行高奖励动作，触发特定条件时执行低奖励动作。
AI.result: 四个任务中，受污染数据训练的智能体在触发器下性能平均下降53.9%-64.7%，且后门在微调后仍存在，主流防御方法难以检测。
AI.conclusion: 现有离线强化学习算法对后门攻击无免疫力，需加强开源数据集的安全防护机制研究。

search_text: BAFFLE: Hiding Backdoors in Offline Reinforcement Learning Datasets BAFFLE：在离线强化学习数据集中隐藏后门 Reinforcement learning (RL) makes an agent learn from trial-and-error experiences gathered during the interaction with the environment. Recently, offline RL has become a popular RL paradigm because it saves the interactions with environments. In offline RL, data providers share large pre-collected datasets, and others can train high-quality agents without interacting with the environments. This paradigm has demonstrated effectiveness in critical tasks like robot control, autonomous driving, etc. However, less attention is paid to investigating the security threats to the offline RL system. This paper focuses on backdoor attacks, where some perturbations are added to the data (observations) such that given normal observations, the agent takes high-rewards actions, and low-reward actions on observations injected with triggers. In this paper, we propose Baffle (Backdoor Attack for Offline Reinforcement Learning), an approach that automatically implants backdoors to RL agents by poisoning the offline RL dataset, and evaluate how different offline RL algorithms react to this attack. Our experiments conducted on four tasks and four offline RL algorithms expose a disquieting fact: none of the existing offline RL algorithms is immune to such a backdoor attack. More specifically, Baffle modifies 10\% of the datasets for four tasks (3 robotic controls and 1 autonomous driving). Agents trained on the poisoned datasets perform well in normal settings. However, when triggers are presented, the agents' performance decreases drastically by 63.2\%, 53.9\%, 64.7\%, and 47.4\% in the four tasks on average. The backdoor still persists after fine-tuning poisoned agents on clean datasets. We further show that the inserted backdoor is also hard to be detected by a popular defensive method. This paper calls attention to developing more effective protection for the open-source offline RL dataset. 强化学习（RL）使智能体从与环境交互过程中收集的试错经验中学习。最近，离线强化学习已经成为一种流行的强化学习范式，因为它节省了与环境的交互。在离线强化学习中，数据提供者共享大量预先收集的数据集，其他人可以在不与环境交互的情况下训练高质量的代理。这种范式在机器人控制、自动驾驶等关键任务中证明了有效性。然而，很少有人关注离线强化学习系统的安全威胁的研究。本文重点关注后门攻击，其中一些扰动被添加到数据（观察）中，以便在给定正常观察的情况下，代理对注入触发器的观察采取高奖励行动，并采取低奖励行动。在本文中，我们提出了 Baffle（离线强化学习的后门攻击），这是一种通过毒害离线 RL 数据集自动向 RL 代理植入后门的方法，并评估不同的离线 RL 算法对此攻击的反应。我们对四个任务和四个离线强化学习算法进行的实验揭示了一个令人不安的事实：现有的离线强化学习算法没有一个能够免受这种后门攻击的影响。更具体地说，Baffle 修改了 4 个任务（3 个机器人控制和 1 个自动驾驶）的 10% 的数据集。在中毒数据集上训练的智能体在正常设置下表现良好。然而，当出现触发器时，代理在这四个任务中的性能平均急剧下降 63.2%、53.9%、64.7% 和 47.4%。在干净的数据集上微调中毒代理后，后门仍然存在。我们进一步表明，插入的后门也很难被流行的防御方法检测到。本文呼吁关注为开源离线强化学习数据集开发更有效的保护。 本文提出BAFFLE方法，在离线强化学习数据集中植入后门，使智能体在正常情况下表现正常，但遇到特定触发器时性能显著下降。 Chen Gong,Zhou Yang,Yunpeng Bai,Junda He,Jieke Shi,Kecen Li,Arunesh Sinha,Bowen Xu,Xinwen Hou,David Lo,Tianhao Wang cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CR

authors_text: Huawei Sun,Lorenzo Servadei,Hao Feng,Michael Stephan,Robert Wille,Avik Santra
title: Utilizing Explainable AI for improving the Performance of Neural Networks
title_zh: 利用可解释的人工智能提高神经网络的性能
abstract: Nowadays, deep neural networks are widely used in a variety of fields that have a direct impact on society. Although those models typically show outstanding performance, they have been used for a long time as black boxes. To address this, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) has been developing as a field that aims to improve the transparency of the model and increase their trustworthiness. We propose a retraining pipeline that consistently improves the model predictions starting from XAI and utilizing state-of-the-art techniques. To do that, we use the XAI results, namely SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) values, to give specific training weights to the data samples. This leads to an improved training of the model and, consequently, better performance. In order to benchmark our method, we evaluate it on both real-life and public datasets. First, we perform the method on a radar-based people counting scenario. Afterward, we test it on the CIFAR-10, a public Computer Vision dataset. Experiments using the SHAP-based retraining approach achieve a 4% more accuracy w.r.t. the standard equal weight retraining for people counting tasks. Moreover, on the CIFAR-10, our SHAP-based weighting strategy ends up with a 3% accuracy rate than the training procedure with equal weighted samples.
summary: Nowadays, deep neural networks are widely used in a variety of fields that have a direct impact on society. Although those models typically show outstanding performance, they have been used for a long time as black boxes. To address this, Explainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) has been developing as a field that aims to improve the transparency of the model and increase their trustworthiness. We propose a retraining pipeline that consistently improves the model predictions starting from XAI and utilizing state-of-the-art techniques. To do that, we use the XAI results, namely SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) values, to give specific training weights to the data samples. This leads to an improved training of the model and, consequently, better performance. In order to benchmark our method, we evaluate it on both real-life and public datasets. First, we perform the method on a radar-based people counting scenario. Afterward, we test it on the CIFAR-10, a public Computer Vision dataset. Experiments using the SHAP-based retraining approach achieve a 4% more accuracy w.r.t. the standard equal weight retraining for people counting tasks. Moreover, on the CIFAR-10, our SHAP-based weighting strategy ends up with a 3% accuracy rate than the training procedure with equal weighted samples.
abstract_zh: 如今，深度神经网络广泛应用于对社会产生直接影响的各个领域。尽管这些模型通常表现出出色的性能，但它们长期以来一直被用作黑匣子。为了解决这个问题，可解释人工智能（XAI）一直在发展成为一个旨在提高模型透明度并增加其可信度的领域。我们提出了一个重新训练管道，从 XAI 开始并利用最先进的技术，不断改进模型预测。为此，我们使用 XAI 结果，即 SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) 值，为数据样本赋予特定的训练权重。这可以改进模型的训练，从而提高性能。为了对我们的方法进行基准测试，我们在现实生活和公共数据集上对其进行了评估。首先，我们在基于雷达的人数统计场景上执行该方法。随后，我们在公共计算机视觉数据集 CIFAR-10 上对其进行了测试。使用基于 SHAP 的再训练方法的实验将准确率提高了 4%。人数统计任务的标准等重再训练。此外，在 CIFAR-10 上，我们基于 SHAP 的加权策略最终的准确率比具有相同加权样本的训练过程高出 3%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种利用可解释人工智能（XAI）技术改进神经网络性能的方法。
AI.motivation: 尽管深度神经网络在多个领域表现出色，但它们通常被视为黑箱模型，缺乏透明度和可信度。因此，需要一种方法来提高模型的可解释性与性能。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种基于XAI的重新训练流程，利用SHAP值为数据样本分配特定的训练权重，以改进模型的训练过程。
AI.result: 在雷达计数任务中，基于SHAP的重新训练方法比标准等权重训练方法提高了4%的准确率；在CIFAR-10数据集上，该方法比等权重训练方法提高了3%的准确率。
AI.conclusion: 基于XAI的重新训练方法能够有效提升神经网络的性能，特别是在需要提高模型透明度和可信度的应用场景中具有重要意义。

authors_text: Ruotian Ma,Xuanting Chen,Lin Zhang,Xin Zhou,Junzhe Wang,Tao Gui,Qi Zhang,Xiang Gao,Yunwen Chen
title: Learning "O" Helps for Learning More: Handling the Concealed Entity Problem for Class-incremental NER
title_zh: 学习“O”有助于了解更多：处理类增量 NER 的隐藏实体问题
abstract: As the categories of named entities rapidly increase, the deployed NER models are required to keep updating toward recognizing more entity types, creating a demand for class-incremental learning for NER. Considering the privacy concerns and storage constraints, the standard paradigm for class-incremental NER updates the models with training data only annotated with the new classes, yet the entities from other entity classes are unlabeled, regarded as "Non-entity" (or "O"). In this work, we conduct an empirical study on the "Unlabeled Entity Problem" and find that it leads to severe confusion between "O" and entities, decreasing class discrimination of old classes and declining the model's ability to learn new classes. To solve the Unlabeled Entity Problem, we propose a novel representation learning method to learn discriminative representations for the entity classes and "O". Specifically, we propose an entity-aware contrastive learning method that adaptively detects entity clusters in "O". Furthermore, we propose two effective distance-based relabeling strategies for better learning the old classes. We introduce a more realistic and challenging benchmark for class-incremental NER, and the proposed method achieves up to 10.62\% improvement over the baseline methods.
summary: As the categories of named entities rapidly increase, the deployed NER models are required to keep updating toward recognizing more entity types, creating a demand for class-incremental learning for NER. Considering the privacy concerns and storage constraints, the standard paradigm for class-incremental NER updates the models with training data only annotated with the new classes, yet the entities from other entity classes are unlabeled, regarded as "Non-entity" (or "O"). In this work, we conduct an empirical study on the "Unlabeled Entity Problem" and find that it leads to severe confusion between "O" and entities, decreasing class discrimination of old classes and declining the model's ability to learn new classes. To solve the Unlabeled Entity Problem, we propose a novel representation learning method to learn discriminative representations for the entity classes and "O". Specifically, we propose an entity-aware contrastive learning method that adaptively detects entity clusters in "O". Furthermore, we propose two effective distance-based relabeling strategies for better learning the old classes. We introduce a more realistic and challenging benchmark for class-incremental NER, and the proposed method achieves up to 10.62\% improvement over the baseline methods.
abstract_zh: 随着命名实体类别的迅速增加，部署的NER模型需要不断更新以识别更多实体类型，从而产生了NER的类增量学习需求。考虑到隐私问题和存储限制，类增量 NER 的标准范例使用仅用新类注释的训练数据更新模型，而来自其他实体类的实体则未标记，被视为“非实体”（或“O”）。在这项工作中，我们对“无标签实体问题”进行了实证研究，发现它导致“O”和实体之间的严重混淆，减少了旧类的类歧视并降低了模型学习新类的能力。为了解决未标记实体问题，我们提出了一种新颖的表示学习方法来学习实体类和“O”的判别表示。具体来说，我们提出了一种实体感知对比学习方法，可以自适应地检测“O”中的实体簇。此外，我们提出了两种有效的基于距离的重新标记策略，以更好地学习旧类。我们为类增量 NER 引入了一个更现实且更具挑战性的基准，所提出的方法比基线方法实现了高达 10.62% 的改进。

AI.tldr: 该论文研究了在类别增量命名实体识别（NER）中如何处理隐藏实体问题，以提高模型对新旧实体类别的识别能力。
AI.motivation: 随着命名实体类别迅速增加，现有的NER模型需要不断更新以识别更多实体类型。然而，由于隐私和存储限制，新类别的训练数据未标注旧类别实体，导致模型在学习新类别时出现混淆。
AI.method: 论文提出了一种实体感知的对比学习方法，用于学习实体类别和“O”（非实体）之间的判别表示，并引入了两种基于距离的重标记策略，以更好地学习旧类别。
AI.result: 论文引入了一个更真实且具有挑战性的类别增量NER基准，并且所提出的方法在基准测试中比基线方法提升了最多10.62%。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效缓解了隐藏实体问题，显著提高了类别增量NER的性能。

search_text: Learning "O" Helps for Learning More: Handling the Concealed Entity Problem for Class-incremental NER 学习“O”有助于了解更多：处理类增量 NER 的隐藏实体问题 As the categories of named entities rapidly increase, the deployed NER models are required to keep updating toward recognizing more entity types, creating a demand for class-incremental learning for NER. Considering the privacy concerns and storage constraints, the standard paradigm for class-incremental NER updates the models with training data only annotated with the new classes, yet the entities from other entity classes are unlabeled, regarded as "Non-entity" (or "O"). In this work, we conduct an empirical study on the "Unlabeled Entity Problem" and find that it leads to severe confusion between "O" and entities, decreasing class discrimination of old classes and declining the model's ability to learn new classes. To solve the Unlabeled Entity Problem, we propose a novel representation learning method to learn discriminative representations for the entity classes and "O". Specifically, we propose an entity-aware contrastive learning method that adaptively detects entity clusters in "O". Furthermore, we propose two effective distance-based relabeling strategies for better learning the old classes. We introduce a more realistic and challenging benchmark for class-incremental NER, and the proposed method achieves up to 10.62\% improvement over the baseline methods. 随着命名实体类别的迅速增加，部署的NER模型需要不断更新以识别更多实体类型，从而产生了NER的类增量学习需求。考虑到隐私问题和存储限制，类增量 NER 的标准范例使用仅用新类注释的训练数据更新模型，而来自其他实体类的实体则未标记，被视为“非实体”（或“O”）。在这项工作中，我们对“无标签实体问题”进行了实证研究，发现它导致“O”和实体之间的严重混淆，减少了旧类的类歧视并降低了模型学习新类的能力。为了解决未标记实体问题，我们提出了一种新颖的表示学习方法来学习实体类和“O”的判别表示。具体来说，我们提出了一种实体感知对比学习方法，可以自适应地检测“O”中的实体簇。此外，我们提出了两种有效的基于距离的重新标记策略，以更好地学习旧类。我们为类增量 NER 引入了一个更现实且更具挑战性的基准，所提出的方法比基线方法实现了高达 10.62% 的改进。 该论文研究了在类别增量命名实体识别（NER）中如何处理隐藏实体问题，以提高模型对新旧实体类别的识别能力。 Ruotian Ma,Xuanting Chen,Lin Zhang,Xin Zhou,Junzhe Wang,Tao Gui,Qi Zhang,Xiang Gao,Yunwen Chen cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Sophie Henning,William Beluch,Alexander Fraser,Annemarie Friedrich
title: A Survey of Methods for Addressing Class Imbalance in Deep-Learning Based Natural Language Processing
title_zh: 基于深度学习的自然语言处理中解决类不平衡的方法综述
abstract: Many natural language processing (NLP) tasks are naturally imbalanced, as some target categories occur much more frequently than others in the real world. In such scenarios, current NLP models still tend to perform poorly on less frequent classes. Addressing class imbalance in NLP is an active research topic, yet, finding a good approach for a particular task and imbalance scenario is difficult. With this survey, the first overview on class imbalance in deep-learning based NLP, we provide guidance for NLP researchers and practitioners dealing with imbalanced data. We first discuss various types of controlled and real-world class imbalance. Our survey then covers approaches that have been explicitly proposed for class-imbalanced NLP tasks or, originating in the computer vision community, have been evaluated on them. We organize the methods by whether they are based on sampling, data augmentation, choice of loss function, staged learning, or model design. Finally, we discuss open problems such as dealing with multi-label scenarios, and propose systematic benchmarking and reporting in order to move forward on this problem as a community.
summary: Many natural language processing (NLP) tasks are naturally imbalanced, as some target categories occur much more frequently than others in the real world. In such scenarios, current NLP models still tend to perform poorly on less frequent classes. Addressing class imbalance in NLP is an active research topic, yet, finding a good approach for a particular task and imbalance scenario is difficult. With this survey, the first overview on class imbalance in deep-learning based NLP, we provide guidance for NLP researchers and practitioners dealing with imbalanced data. We first discuss various types of controlled and real-world class imbalance. Our survey then covers approaches that have been explicitly proposed for class-imbalanced NLP tasks or, originating in the computer vision community, have been evaluated on them. We organize the methods by whether they are based on sampling, data augmentation, choice of loss function, staged learning, or model design. Finally, we discuss open problems such as dealing with multi-label scenarios, and propose systematic benchmarking and reporting in order to move forward on this problem as a community.
abstract_zh: 许多自然语言处理 (NLP) 任务本质上是不平衡的，因为某些目标类别在现实世界中比其他目标类别出现的频率要高得多。在这种情况下，当前的 NLP 模型在频率较低的类别上仍然表现不佳。解决 NLP 中的类别不平衡是一个活跃的研究课题，然而，为特定任务和不平衡场景找到一个好的方法是很困难的。通过这项调查，我们首次概述了基于深度学习的 NLP 中的类别不平衡，为处理不平衡数据的 NLP 研究人员和从业者提供了指导。我们首先讨论各种类型的受控和现实世界的阶级不平衡。然后，我们的调查涵盖了针对类不平衡 NLP 任务明确提出的方法，或者源自计算机视觉社区的方法，并对其进行了评估。我们根据是否基于采样、数据增强、损失函数的选择、分阶段学习或模型设计来组织这些方法。最后，我们讨论了诸如处理多标签场景之类的开放问题，并提出了系统的基准测试和报告，以便作为一个社区来解决这个问题。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了深度学习在自然语言处理中处理类别不平衡问题的方法，为研究者和实践者提供指导。
AI.motivation: 自然语言处理任务中类别不平衡现象普遍，导致模型在少数类上表现不佳，但现有方法缺乏系统性指导。
AI.method: 通过分类讨论控制型和真实场景不平衡，涵盖采样、数据增强、损失函数设计等方法，并提出多标签场景的开放问题及基准测试建议。
AI.result: 总结了针对类别不平衡的主流方法，指出现有研究的不足，并提出系统化评估框架以推动领域发展。
AI.conclusion: 类别不平衡是NLP中的关键挑战，需进一步研究多标签场景解决方案，并建立统一基准以促进方法比较与优化。

authors_text: Caio Azevedo,Thomas Gilles,Stefano Sabatini,Dzmitry Tsishkou
title: Exploiting map information for self-supervised learning in motion forecasting
title_zh: 利用地图信息进行运动预测中的自监督学习
abstract: Inspired by recent developments regarding the application of self-supervised learning (SSL), we devise an auxiliary task for trajectory prediction that takes advantage of map-only information such as graph connectivity with the intent of improving map comprehension and generalization. We apply this auxiliary task through two frameworks - multitasking and pretraining. In either framework we observe significant improvement of our baseline in metrics such as $\mathrm{minFDE}_6$ (as much as 20.3%) and $\mathrm{MissRate}_6$ (as much as 33.3%), as well as a richer comprehension of map features demonstrated by different training configurations. The results obtained were consistent in all three data sets used for experiments: Argoverse, Interaction and NuScenes. We also submit our new pretrained model's results to the Interaction challenge and achieve $\textit{1st}$ place with respect to $\mathrm{minFDE}_6$ and $\mathrm{minADE}_6$.
summary: Inspired by recent developments regarding the application of self-supervised learning (SSL), we devise an auxiliary task for trajectory prediction that takes advantage of map-only information such as graph connectivity with the intent of improving map comprehension and generalization. We apply this auxiliary task through two frameworks - multitasking and pretraining. In either framework we observe significant improvement of our baseline in metrics such as $\mathrm{minFDE}_6$ (as much as 20.3%) and $\mathrm{MissRate}_6$ (as much as 33.3%), as well as a richer comprehension of map features demonstrated by different training configurations. The results obtained were consistent in all three data sets used for experiments: Argoverse, Interaction and NuScenes. We also submit our new pretrained model's results to the Interaction challenge and achieve $\textit{1st}$ place with respect to $\mathrm{minFDE}_6$ and $\mathrm{minADE}_6$.
abstract_zh: 受自监督学习（SSL）应用最新发展的启发，我们设计了一项轨迹预测辅助任务，该任务利用仅地图信息（例如图连接性），旨在提高地图理解和泛化能力。我们通过两个框架应用这个辅助任务——多任务处理和预训练。在这两个框架中，我们观察到 $\mathrm{minFDE}_6$（高达 20.3%）和 $\mathrm{MissRate}_6$（高达 33.3%）等指标的基线有了显着改善，并且通过不同的训练配置展示了对地图特征的更丰富的理解。实验所用的所有三个数据集（Argoverse、Interaction 和 NuScenes）获得的结果都是一致的。我们还将新的预训练模型的结果提交给交互挑战，并在 $\mathrm{minFDE}_6$ 和 $\mathrm{minADE}_6$ 方面获得 $\textit{1st}$ 名次。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用地图信息进行自监督学习，以提升运动预测任务中的轨迹预测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有轨迹预测方法依赖标注数据，而标注数据获取成本高且泛化性差。本文旨在通过自监督学习利用地图结构信息，提升模型对地图特征的理解和预测泛化能力。
AI.method: 设计基于地图图连接性的辅助任务，通过多任务学习和预训练框架整合到主预测任务中，无需额外标注数据即可学习地图特征表示。
AI.result: 在Argoverse、Interaction和NuScenes三个数据集上，minFDE6指标提升最高达20.3%，MissRate6提升33.3%，并在Interaction挑战赛中取得minFDE6和minADE6两项冠军。
AI.conclusion: 融合地图结构的自监督学习能显著提升轨迹预测性能，证明了地图先验知识对运动预测任务的有效性与泛化性。

ai_content_json.result: 在Argoverse、Interaction和NuScenes三个数据集上，minFDE6指标提升最高达20.3%，MissRate6提升33.3%，并在Interaction挑战赛中取得minFDE6和minADE6两项冠军。
ai_content_json.conclusion: 融合地图结构的自监督学习能显著提升轨迹预测性能，证明了地图先验知识对运动预测任务的有效性与泛化性。

tldr: 本文提出利用地图信息进行自监督学习，以提升运动预测任务中的轨迹预测性能。
motivation: 现有轨迹预测方法依赖标注数据，而标注数据获取成本高且泛化性差。本文旨在通过自监督学习利用地图结构信息，提升模型对地图特征的理解和预测泛化能力。
method: 设计基于地图图连接性的辅助任务，通过多任务学习和预训练框架整合到主预测任务中，无需额外标注数据即可学习地图特征表示。
result: 在Argoverse、Interaction和NuScenes三个数据集上，minFDE6指标提升最高达20.3%，MissRate6提升33.3%，并在Interaction挑战赛中取得minFDE6和minADE6两项冠军。
conclusion: 融合地图结构的自监督学习能显著提升轨迹预测性能，证明了地图先验知识对运动预测任务的有效性与泛化性。
search_text: Exploiting map information for self-supervised learning in motion forecasting 利用地图信息进行运动预测中的自监督学习 Inspired by recent developments regarding the application of self-supervised learning (SSL), we devise an auxiliary task for trajectory prediction that takes advantage of map-only information such as graph connectivity with the intent of improving map comprehension and generalization. We apply this auxiliary task through two frameworks - multitasking and pretraining. In either framework we observe significant improvement of our baseline in metrics such as $\mathrm{minFDE}_6$ (as much as 20.3%) and $\mathrm{MissRate}_6$ (as much as 33.3%), as well as a richer comprehension of map features demonstrated by different training configurations. The results obtained were consistent in all three data sets used for experiments: Argoverse, Interaction and NuScenes. We also submit our new pretrained model's results to the Interaction challenge and achieve $\textit{1st}$ place with respect to $\mathrm{minFDE}_6$ and $\mathrm{minADE}_6$. 受自监督学习（SSL）应用最新发展的启发，我们设计了一项轨迹预测辅助任务，该任务利用仅地图信息（例如图连接性），旨在提高地图理解和泛化能力。我们通过两个框架应用这个辅助任务——多任务处理和预训练。在这两个框架中，我们观察到 $\mathrm{minFDE}_6$（高达 20.3%）和 $\mathrm{MissRate}_6$（高达 33.3%）等指标的基线有了显着改善，并且通过不同的训练配置展示了对地图特征的更丰富的理解。实验所用的所有三个数据集（Argoverse、Interaction 和 NuScenes）获得的结果都是一致的。我们还将新的预训练模型的结果提交给交互挑战，并在 $\mathrm{minFDE}_6$ 和 $\mathrm{minADE}_6$ 方面获得 $\textit{1st}$ 名次。 本文提出利用地图信息进行自监督学习，以提升运动预测任务中的轨迹预测性能。 Caio Azevedo,Thomas Gilles,Stefano Sabatini,Dzmitry Tsishkou cs.CV

authors_text: Yujie Zhang,Qi Yang,Yifei Zhou,Xiaozhong Xu,Le Yang,Yiling Xu
title: TCDM: Transformational Complexity Based Distortion Metric for Perceptual Point Cloud Quality Assessment
title_zh: TCDM：用于感知点云质量评估的基于变换复杂性的失真度量
abstract: The goal of objective point cloud quality assessment (PCQA) research is to develop quantitative metrics that measure point cloud quality in a perceptually consistent manner. Merging the research of cognitive science and intuition of the human visual system (HVS), in this paper, we evaluate the point cloud quality by measuring the complexity of transforming the distorted point cloud back to its reference, which in practice can be approximated by the code length of one point cloud when the other is given. For this purpose, we first make space segmentation for the reference and distorted point clouds based on a 3D Voronoi diagram to obtain a series of local patch pairs. Next, inspired by the predictive coding theory, we utilize a space-aware vector autoregressive (SA-VAR) model to encode the geometry and color channels of each reference patch with and without the distorted patch, respectively. Assuming that the residual errors follow the multi-variate Gaussian distributions, the self-complexity of the reference and transformational complexity between the reference and distorted samples are computed using covariance matrices. Additionally, the prediction terms generated by SA-VAR are introduced as one auxiliary feature to promote the final quality prediction. The effectiveness of the proposed transformational complexity based distortion metric (TCDM) is evaluated through extensive experiments conducted on five public point cloud quality assessment databases. The results demonstrate that TCDM achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance, and further analysis confirms its robustness in various scenarios. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/zyj1318053/TCDM.
summary: The goal of objective point cloud quality assessment (PCQA) research is to develop quantitative metrics that measure point cloud quality in a perceptually consistent manner. Merging the research of cognitive science and intuition of the human visual system (HVS), in this paper, we evaluate the point cloud quality by measuring the complexity of transforming the distorted point cloud back to its reference, which in practice can be approximated by the code length of one point cloud when the other is given. For this purpose, we first make space segmentation for the reference and distorted point clouds based on a 3D Voronoi diagram to obtain a series of local patch pairs. Next, inspired by the predictive coding theory, we utilize a space-aware vector autoregressive (SA-VAR) model to encode the geometry and color channels of each reference patch with and without the distorted patch, respectively. Assuming that the residual errors follow the multi-variate Gaussian distributions, the self-complexity of the reference and transformational complexity between the reference and distorted samples are computed using covariance matrices. Additionally, the prediction terms generated by SA-VAR are introduced as one auxiliary feature to promote the final quality prediction. The effectiveness of the proposed transformational complexity based distortion metric (TCDM) is evaluated through extensive experiments conducted on five public point cloud quality assessment databases. The results demonstrate that TCDM achieves state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance, and further analysis confirms its robustness in various scenarios. The code is publicly available at https://github.com/zyj1318053/TCDM.
abstract_zh: 客观点云质量评估（PCQA）研究的目标是开发以感知一致的方式测量点云质量的定量指标。结合认知科学和人类视觉系统（HVS）直觉的研究，本文通过测量将扭曲的点云转换回其参考点的复杂性来评估点云质量，在实践中可以通过给定一个点云时另一个点云的代码长度来近似。为此，我们首先基于3D Voronoi图对参考点云和扭曲点云进行空间分割，以获得一系列局部补丁对。接下来，受预测编码理论的启发，我们利用空间感知向量自回归（SA-VAR）模型分别对有和没有扭曲补丁的每个参考补丁的几何和颜色通道进行编码。假设残差遵循多元高斯分布，则使用协方差矩阵计算参考的自复杂度以及参考样本和失真样本之间的变换复杂度。此外，引入了 SA-VAR 生成的预测项作为一种辅助特征，以促进最终的质量预测。通过在五个公共点云质量评估数据库上进行的广泛实验来评估所提出的基于转换复杂性的失真度量（TCDM）的有效性。结果表明 TCDM 实现了最先进的 (SOTA) 性能，并且进一步分析证实了其在各种场景中的鲁棒性。该代码可在 https://github.com/zyj1318053/TCDM 上公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于变换复杂度的点云质量评估方法TCDM，通过测量失真点云恢复到参考点云的复杂度来评估感知质量。
AI.motivation: 现有客观点云质量评估方法未能充分结合人类视觉系统的认知特性，需开发更符合感知一致性的量化指标。
AI.method: 基于3D Voronoi图分割点云局部区域，利用空间感知向量自回归模型（SA-VAR）编码几何与颜色信息，通过协方差矩阵计算参考点云自复杂度及参考与失真点云间的变换复杂度，并引入预测项作为辅助特征。
AI.result: 在五个公开数据集上的实验表明，TCDM在点云质量评估任务中达到最先进水平，且在多种场景下具有鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: TCDM通过结合认知科学与预测编码理论，有效提升了点云质量评估的感知一致性，代码已开源。

authors_text: Haoxiang Guo
title: Depth-First Grover Search Algorithm on Hybrid Quantum-Classical Computer
title_zh: 混合量子经典计算机上的深度优先 Grover 搜索算法
abstract: We demonstrated the detailed construction of the hybrid quantum-classical computer. Based on this architecture, the useful concept of amplitude interception is illustrated. It is then embedded into a combination of Depth-First Search and Grover's algorithm to generate a novel approach, the Depth-First Grover Search(DFGS), to handle multi-solution searching problems on unstructured databases with an unknown number of solutions. Our new algorithm attains an average complexity of $\mathcal{O}(m\sqrt{N})$ which performs as efficient as a normal Grover Search, and a $\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{p}N)$ complexity with a manually determined constant $p$ for the case with all elements are solutions, where a normal Grover Search will degenerate to $\mathcal{O}(N\sqrt{N})$. The DFGS algorithm is more robust and stable in comparison.
summary: We demonstrated the detailed construction of the hybrid quantum-classical computer. Based on this architecture, the useful concept of amplitude interception is illustrated. It is then embedded into a combination of Depth-First Search and Grover's algorithm to generate a novel approach, the Depth-First Grover Search(DFGS), to handle multi-solution searching problems on unstructured databases with an unknown number of solutions. Our new algorithm attains an average complexity of $\mathcal{O}(m\sqrt{N})$ which performs as efficient as a normal Grover Search, and a $\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{p}N)$ complexity with a manually determined constant $p$ for the case with all elements are solutions, where a normal Grover Search will degenerate to $\mathcal{O}(N\sqrt{N})$. The DFGS algorithm is more robust and stable in comparison.
abstract_zh: 我们演示了混合量子经典计算机的详细构造。基于该架构，说明了幅度截取的有用概念。然后将其嵌入到深度优先搜索和 Grover 算法的组合中，生成一种新颖的方法，即深度优先 Grover 搜索 (DFGS)，用于处理具有未知数量解决方案的非结构化数据库上的多解决方案搜索问题。我们的新算法的平均复杂度为 $\mathcal{O}(m\sqrt{N})$，其执行效率与普通 Grover 搜索一样高效，并且在所有元素都是解的情况下，手动确定常数 $p$ 的复杂度为 $\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{p}N)$，其中普通 Grover 搜索将退化为 $\mathcal{O}(N\sqrt{N})$。相比之下，DFGS算法更加鲁棒和稳定。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于混合量子-经典计算机的深度优先Grover搜索算法（DFGS），用于高效解决无结构数据库中的多解搜索问题。
AI.motivation: 传统Grover算法在处理所有元素均为解的情况时效率低下，且无法有效应对未知解数量的多解搜索问题，需设计更高效稳定的算法。
AI.method: 通过构建混合量子-经典计算机架构，结合深度优先搜索与Grover算法，并引入振幅拦截技术，设计出DFGS算法以处理多解搜索问题。
AI.result: DFGS算法在平均情况下的时间复杂度为O(m√N)，与传统Grover相当；在全解情况下复杂度为O(√p N)，显著优于传统算法的O(N√N)。
AI.conclusion: DFGS算法在处理多解搜索问题时具有更高的效率和稳定性，尤其适用于解数量未知的无结构数据库场景。

It is then embedded into a combination of Depth-First Search and Grover's algorithm to generate a novel approach, the Depth-First Grover Search(DFGS), to handle multi-solution searching problems on unstructured databases with an unknown number of solutions. Our new algorithm attains an average complexity of $\mathcal{O}(m\sqrt{N})$ which performs as efficient as a normal Grover Search, and a $\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{p}N)$ complexity with a manually determined constant $p$ for the case with all elements are solutions, where a normal Grover Search will degenerate to $\mathcal{O}(N\sqrt{N})$. The DFGS algorithm is more robust and stable in comparison. 我们演示了混合量子经典计算机的详细构造。基于该架构，说明了幅度截取的有用概念。然后将其嵌入到深度优先搜索和 Grover 算法的组合中，生成一种新颖的方法，即深度优先 Grover 搜索 (DFGS)，用于处理具有未知数量解决方案的非结构化数据库上的多解决方案搜索问题。我们的新算法的平均复杂度为 $\mathcal{O}(m\sqrt{N})$，其执行效率与普通 Grover 搜索一样高效，并且在所有元素都是解的情况下，手动确定常数 $p$ 的复杂度为 $\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{p}N)$，其中普通 Grover 搜索将退化为 $\mathcal{O}(N\sqrt{N})$。相比之下，DFGS算法更加鲁棒和稳定。 本文提出了一种基于混合量子-经典计算机的深度优先Grover搜索算法（DFGS），用于高效解决无结构数据库中的多解搜索问题。 Haoxiang Guo quant-ph,cs.DS

authors_text: Fotios Logothetis,Roberto Mecca,Ignas Budvytis,Roberto Cipolla
title: A CNN Based Approach for the Point-Light Photometric Stereo Problem
title_zh: 基于 CNN 的点光光度立体问题方法
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2009.05792
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2009.05792
abstract: Reconstructing the 3D shape of an object using several images under different light sources is a very challenging task, especially when realistic assumptions such as light propagation and attenuation, perspective viewing geometry and specular light reflection are considered. Many of works tackling Photometric Stereo (PS) problems often relax most of the aforementioned assumptions. Especially they ignore specular reflection and global illumination effects. In this work, we propose a CNN-based approach capable of handling these realistic assumptions by leveraging recent improvements of deep neural networks for far-field Photometric Stereo and adapt them to the point light setup. We achieve this by employing an iterative procedure of point-light PS for shape estimation which has two main steps. Firstly we train a per-pixel CNN to predict surface normals from reflectance samples. Secondly, we compute the depth by integrating the normal field in order to iteratively estimate light directions and attenuation which is used to compensate the input images to compute reflectance samples for the next iteration. Our approach sigificantly outperforms the state-of-the-art on the DiLiGenT real world dataset. Furthermore, in order to measure the performance of our approach for near-field point-light source PS data, we introduce LUCES the first real-world 'dataset for near-fieLd point light soUrCe photomEtric Stereo' of 14 objects of different materials were the effects of point light sources and perspective viewing are a lot more significant. Our approach also outperforms the competition on this dataset as well. Data and test code are available at the project page.

Many of works tackling Photometric Stereo (PS) problems often relax most of the aforementioned assumptions. Especially they ignore specular reflection and global illumination effects. In this work, we propose a CNN-based approach capable of handling these realistic assumptions by leveraging recent improvements of deep neural networks for far-field Photometric Stereo and adapt them to the point light setup. We achieve this by employing an iterative procedure of point-light PS for shape estimation which has two main steps. Firstly we train a per-pixel CNN to predict surface normals from reflectance samples. Secondly, we compute the depth by integrating the normal field in order to iteratively estimate light directions and attenuation which is used to compensate the input images to compute reflectance samples for the next iteration. Our approach sigificantly outperforms the state-of-the-art on the DiLiGenT real world dataset. Furthermore, in order to measure the performance of our approach for near-field point-light source PS data, we introduce LUCES the first real-world 'dataset for near-fieLd point light soUrCe photomEtric Stereo' of 14 objects of different materials were the effects of point light sources and perspective viewing are a lot more significant. Our approach also outperforms the competition on this dataset as well. Data and test code are available at the project page.
abstract_zh: 使用不同光源下的多个图像重建物体的 3D 形状是一项非常具有挑战性的任务，特别是在考虑光传播和衰减、透视几何形状和镜面光反射等现实假设时。许多解决光度立体（PS）问题的工作通常会放宽上述大部分假设。特别是它们忽略了镜面反射和全局照明效果。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种基于 CNN 的方法，能够利用最近对远场光度立体的深度神经网络的改进来处理这些现实假设，并使它们适应点光源设置。我们通过采用点光 PS 迭代过程进行形状估计来实现这一点，该过程有两个主要步骤。首先，我们训练每像素 CNN 来根据反射率样本预测表面法线。其次，我们通过积分法线场来计算深度，以便迭代估计光方向和衰减，用于补偿输入图像以计算下一次迭代的反射率样本。我们的方法明显优于 DiLiGenT 现实世界数据集上最先进的方法。此外，为了测量我们的近场点光源PS数据方法的性能，我们引入了LUCES，这是第一个真实世界的“近场点光源光度立体数据集”，包含14个不同材料的物体，点光源和透视观察的效果更加显着。我们的方法在该数据集上也优于竞争对手。数据和测试代码可在项目页面获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于卷积神经网络（CNN）的方法，用于解决点光源光度立体问题，以重建物体的3D形状。
AI.motivation: 传统的光度立体方法通常忽略现实中的复杂因素，如镜面反射和全局光照效应，因此难以准确重建物体的3D形状。
AI.method: 该方法利用深度神经网络，通过迭代过程进行形状估计。首先，使用CNN预测每个像素的表面法线；其次，通过积分法线场计算深度，并迭代估计光源方向和衰减，以补偿输入图像并生成下一迭代的反射样本。
AI.result: 该方法在DiLiGenT真实数据集和新提出的LUCES数据集上均显著优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的方法能够有效处理现实中的复杂光照条件，为近场点光源光度立体问题提供了新的解决方案。

authors_text: Michael Kleinman,Alessandro Achille,Stefano Soatto
title: Critical Learning Periods for Multisensory Integration in Deep Networks
title_zh: 深度网络中多感官整合的关键学习时期
abstract: We show that the ability of a neural network to integrate information from diverse sources hinges critically on being exposed to properly correlated signals during the early phases of training. Interfering with the learning process during this initial stage can permanently impair the development of a skill, both in artificial and biological systems where the phenomenon is known as a critical learning period. We show that critical periods arise from the complex and unstable early transient dynamics, which are decisive of final performance of the trained system and their learned representations. This evidence challenges the view, engendered by analysis of wide and shallow networks, that early learning dynamics of neural networks are simple, akin to those of a linear model. Indeed, we show that even deep linear networks exhibit critical learning periods for multi-source integration, while shallow networks do not. To better understand how the internal representations change according to disturbances or sensory deficits, we introduce a new measure of source sensitivity, which allows us to track the inhibition and integration of sources during training. Our analysis of inhibition suggests cross-source reconstruction as a natural auxiliary training objective, and indeed we show that architectures trained with cross-sensor reconstruction objectives are remarkably more resilient to critical periods. Our findings suggest that the recent success in self-supervised multi-modal training compared to previous supervised efforts may be in part due to more robust learning dynamics and not solely due to better architectures and/or more data.
summary: We show that the ability of a neural network to integrate information from diverse sources hinges critically on being exposed to properly correlated signals during the early phases of training. Interfering with the learning process during this initial stage can permanently impair the development of a skill, both in artificial and biological systems where the phenomenon is known as a critical learning period. We show that critical periods arise from the complex and unstable early transient dynamics, which are decisive of final performance of the trained system and their learned representations. This evidence challenges the view, engendered by analysis of wide and shallow networks, that early learning dynamics of neural networks are simple, akin to those of a linear model. Indeed, we show that even deep linear networks exhibit critical learning periods for multi-source integration, while shallow networks do not. To better understand how the internal representations change according to disturbances or sensory deficits, we introduce a new measure of source sensitivity, which allows us to track the inhibition and integration of sources during training. Our analysis of inhibition suggests cross-source reconstruction as a natural auxiliary training objective, and indeed we show that architectures trained with cross-sensor reconstruction objectives are remarkably more resilient to critical periods. Our findings suggest that the recent success in self-supervised multi-modal training compared to previous supervised efforts may be in part due to more robust learning dynamics and not solely due to better architectures and/or more data.
abstract_zh: 我们表明，神经网络整合来自不同来源的信息的能力关键取决于在训练的早期阶段接触到正确相关的信号。在这个初始阶段干扰学习过程可能会永久损害技能的发展，无论是在人工系统还是生物系统中，这种现象被称为关键学习期。我们表明，关键时期是由复杂且不稳定的早期瞬态动态引起的，这对于训练系统的最终性能及其学习表示具有决定性作用。这一证据挑战了通过分析宽层和浅层网络而产生的观点，即神经网络的早期学习动态很简单，类似于线性模型。事实上，我们表明，即使是深层线性网络也表现出多源集成的关键学习期，而浅层网络则不然。为了更好地理解内部表征如何根据干扰或感觉缺陷而变化，我们引入了一种新的源敏感性测量方法，它使我们能够跟踪训练期间源的抑制和整合。我们对抑制的分析表明，跨源重建是一种自然的辅助训练目标，事实上，我们表明，使用跨传感器重建目标训练的架构对于关键时期的恢复能力明显更强。我们的研究结果表明，与之前的监督工作相比，最近自监督多模式训练的成功可能部分归因于更强大的学习动态，而不仅仅是归因于更好的架构和/或更多数据。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了深度网络中多感官整合的关键学习期现象，探讨了早期训练阶段对网络整合能力的影响。
AI.motivation: 作者旨在揭示神经网络在整合多源信息时，其能力与早期训练阶段的信号相关性密切相关，并探讨关键学习期的成因及其对网络性能的影响。
AI.method: 作者通过分析早期训练阶段的动态变化，引入了源敏感度的新度量方法，并提出跨传感器重建作为辅助训练目标，以增强网络对关键学习期的鲁棒性。
AI.result: 研究发现，深度网络存在关键学习期，而浅层网络则没有。跨传感器重建目标显著提升了网络对关键学习期的抵抗能力，并解释了自监督多模态训练优于传统监督方法的部分原因。
AI.conclusion: 关键学习期的发现挑战了传统线性模型对早期学习动态的简单化理解，并表明自监督训练的成功可能与更稳健的学习动态有关。

To better understand how the internal representations change according to disturbances or sensory deficits, we introduce a new measure of source sensitivity, which allows us to track the inhibition and integration of sources during training. Our analysis of inhibition suggests cross-source reconstruction as a natural auxiliary training objective, and indeed we show that architectures trained with cross-sensor reconstruction objectives are remarkably more resilient to critical periods. Our findings suggest that the recent success in self-supervised multi-modal training compared to previous supervised efforts may be in part due to more robust learning dynamics and not solely due to better architectures and/or more data. 我们表明，神经网络整合来自不同来源的信息的能力关键取决于在训练的早期阶段接触到正确相关的信号。在这个初始阶段干扰学习过程可能会永久损害技能的发展，无论是在人工系统还是生物系统中，这种现象被称为关键学习期。我们表明，关键时期是由复杂且不稳定的早期瞬态动态引起的，这对于训练系统的最终性能及其学习表示具有决定性作用。这一证据挑战了通过分析宽层和浅层网络而产生的观点，即神经网络的早期学习动态很简单，类似于线性模型。事实上，我们表明，即使是深层线性网络也表现出多源集成的关键学习期，而浅层网络则不然。为了更好地理解内部表征如何根据干扰或感觉缺陷而变化，我们引入了一种新的源敏感性测量方法，它使我们能够跟踪训练期间源的抑制和整合。我们对抑制的分析表明，跨源重建是一种自然的辅助训练目标，事实上，我们表明，使用跨传感器重建目标训练的架构对于关键时期的恢复能力明显更强。我们的研究结果表明，与之前的监督工作相比，最近自监督多模式训练的成功可能部分归因于更强大的学习动态，而不仅仅是归因于更好的架构和/或更多数据。 本文研究了深度网络中多感官整合的关键学习期现象，探讨了早期训练阶段对网络整合能力的影响。 Michael Kleinman,Alessandro Achille,Stefano Soatto cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CV,q-bio.NC

authors_text: Viraj Mehta,Ian Char,Joseph Abbate,Rory Conlin,Mark D. Boyer,Stefano Ermon,Jeff Schneider,Willie Neiswanger
title: Exploration via Planning for Information about the Optimal Trajectory
title_zh: 通过规划探索最佳轨迹信息
comment: Conference paper at Neurips 2022. Code available at https://github.com/fusion-ml/trajectory-information-rl. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2112.05244
comments: Conference paper at Neurips 2022. Code available at https://github.com/fusion-ml/trajectory-information-rl. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2112.05244
abstract: Many potential applications of reinforcement learning (RL) are stymied by the large numbers of samples required to learn an effective policy. This is especially true when applying RL to real-world control tasks, e.g. in the sciences or robotics, where executing a policy in the environment is costly. In popular RL algorithms, agents typically explore either by adding stochasticity to a reward-maximizing policy or by attempting to gather maximal information about environment dynamics without taking the given task into account. In this work, we develop a method that allows us to plan for exploration while taking both the task and the current knowledge about the dynamics into account. The key insight to our approach is to plan an action sequence that maximizes the expected information gain about the optimal trajectory for the task at hand. We demonstrate that our method learns strong policies with 2x fewer samples than strong exploration baselines and 200x fewer samples than model free methods on a diverse set of low-to-medium dimensional control tasks in both the open-loop and closed-loop control settings.
summary: Many potential applications of reinforcement learning (RL) are stymied by the large numbers of samples required to learn an effective policy. This is especially true when applying RL to real-world control tasks, e.g. in the sciences or robotics, where executing a policy in the environment is costly. In popular RL algorithms, agents typically explore either by adding stochasticity to a reward-maximizing policy or by attempting to gather maximal information about environment dynamics without taking the given task into account. In this work, we develop a method that allows us to plan for exploration while taking both the task and the current knowledge about the dynamics into account. The key insight to our approach is to plan an action sequence that maximizes the expected information gain about the optimal trajectory for the task at hand. We demonstrate that our method learns strong policies with 2x fewer samples than strong exploration baselines and 200x fewer samples than model free methods on a diverse set of low-to-medium dimensional control tasks in both the open-loop and closed-loop control settings.
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）的许多潜在应用都因学习有效策略所需的大量样本而受到阻碍。当将强化学习应用于现实世界的控制任务时尤其如此，例如在科学或机器人技术中，在环境中执行政策的成本很高。在流行的强化学习算法中，智能体通常通过在奖励最大化策略中添加随机性或尝试在不考虑给定任务的情况下收集有关环境动态的最大信息来进行探索。在这项工作中，我们开发了一种方法，使我们能够在考虑任务和当前动态知识的同时规划探索。我们方法的关键见解是规划一个行动序列，以最大化有关当前任务的最佳轨迹的预期信息增益。我们证明，在开环和闭环控制设置中，我们的方法在一组不同的中低维控制任务上学习强大的策略，其样本数比强探索基线少 2 倍，比无模型方法少 200 倍。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合任务目标和动态知识的探索规划方法，通过最大化预期信息增益来优化强化学习中的样本效率。
AI.motivation: 现有强化学习方法在现实控制任务中面临样本需求高、探索效率低的问题，传统探索策略要么依赖随机性，要么忽视任务目标，导致资源浪费。
AI.method: 设计基于规划的探索框架，通过动态模型预测动作序列对最优轨迹信息的增益，优先选择能提升任务相关知识的探索路径。
AI.result: 在多个低至中等维度控制任务中，该方法相比强探索基线减少2倍样本，较无模型方法减少200倍样本，展现显著效率优势。
AI.conclusion: 规划驱动的信息增益最大化探索策略有效平衡了任务目标与动态学习，为高效强化学习提供了新范式。

authors_text: Jiayi Shen,Zehao Xiao,Xiantong Zhen,Cees G. M. Snoek,Marcel Worring
title: Association Graph Learning for Multi-Task Classification with Category Shifts
title_zh: 用于具有类别转换的多任务分类的关联图学习
abstract: In this paper, we focus on multi-task classification, where related classification tasks share the same label space and are learned simultaneously. In particular, we tackle a new setting, which is more realistic than currently addressed in the literature, where categories shift from training to test data. Hence, individual tasks do not contain complete training data for the categories in the test set. To generalize to such test data, it is crucial for individual tasks to leverage knowledge from related tasks. To this end, we propose learning an association graph to transfer knowledge among tasks for missing classes. We construct the association graph with nodes representing tasks, classes and instances, and encode the relationships among the nodes in the edges to guide their mutual knowledge transfer. By message passing on the association graph, our model enhances the categorical information of each instance, making it more discriminative. To avoid spurious correlations between task and class nodes in the graph, we introduce an assignment entropy maximization that encourages each class node to balance its edge weights. This enables all tasks to fully utilize the categorical information from related tasks. An extensive evaluation on three general benchmarks and a medical dataset for skin lesion classification reveals that our method consistently performs better than representative baselines.
summary: In this paper, we focus on multi-task classification, where related classification tasks share the same label space and are learned simultaneously. In particular, we tackle a new setting, which is more realistic than currently addressed in the literature, where categories shift from training to test data. Hence, individual tasks do not contain complete training data for the categories in the test set. To generalize to such test data, it is crucial for individual tasks to leverage knowledge from related tasks. To this end, we propose learning an association graph to transfer knowledge among tasks for missing classes. We construct the association graph with nodes representing tasks, classes and instances, and encode the relationships among the nodes in the edges to guide their mutual knowledge transfer. By message passing on the association graph, our model enhances the categorical information of each instance, making it more discriminative. To avoid spurious correlations between task and class nodes in the graph, we introduce an assignment entropy maximization that encourages each class node to balance its edge weights. This enables all tasks to fully utilize the categorical information from related tasks. An extensive evaluation on three general benchmarks and a medical dataset for skin lesion classification reveals that our method consistently performs better than representative baselines.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们关注多任务分类，其中相关的分类任务共享相同的标签空间并同时学习。特别是，我们处理了一个新的设置，它比目前文献中提到的更现实，其中类别从训练数据转移到测试数据。因此，各个任务不包含测试集中类别的完整训练数据。为了推广到此类测试数据，各个任务利用相关任务中的知识至关重要。为此，我们建议学习关联图以在缺失类的任务之间转移知识。我们用代表任务、类和实例的节点构建关联图，并对边中节点之间的关系进行编码以指导它们的相互知识转移。通过在关联图上传递消息，我们的模型增强了每个实例的分类信息，使其更具辨别力。为了避免图中任务和类节点之间的虚假相关性，我们引入了分配熵最大化，鼓励每个类节点平衡其边缘权重。这使得所有任务都能充分利用相关任务的分类信息。对三个通用基准和皮肤病变分类医学数据集的广泛评估表明，我们的方法始终比代表性基线表现更好。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于多任务分类的关联图学习方法，以应对测试数据中类别偏移的问题。
AI.motivation: 在多任务分类中，测试数据中的类别可能会发生偏移，导致单个任务无法获得完整的训练数据，因此需要从相关任务中转移知识以提高泛化能力。
AI.method: 本文提出通过构建一个关联图，其中节点表示任务、类别和实例，并通过边编码节点之间的关系来指导知识的相互转移。通过图上的消息传递增强实例的类别信息，并引入分配熵最大化以避免任务与类别节点之间的虚假相关性。
AI.result: 在三个通用基准和一个皮肤病变分类的医学数据集上的广泛评估表明，该方法在代表性基线方法上表现更优。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过关联图学习有效利用相关任务的知识，提高了在类别偏移情况下的多任务分类性能。

authors_text: Nuo Chen,Qiushi Sun,Renyu Zhu,Xiang Li,Xuesong Lu,Ming Gao
title: CAT-probing: A Metric-based Approach to Interpret How Pre-trained Models for Programming Language Attend Code Structure
title_zh: CAT 探测：一种基于度量的方法来解释编程语言的预训练模型如何参与代码结构
abstract: Code pre-trained models (CodePTMs) have recently demonstrated significant success in code intelligence. To interpret these models, some probing methods have been applied. However, these methods fail to consider the inherent characteristics of codes. In this paper, to address the problem, we propose a novel probing method CAT-probing to quantitatively interpret how CodePTMs attend code structure. We first denoise the input code sequences based on the token types pre-defined by the compilers to filter those tokens whose attention scores are too small. After that, we define a new metric CAT-score to measure the commonality between the token-level attention scores generated in CodePTMs and the pair-wise distances between corresponding AST nodes. The higher the CAT-score, the stronger the ability of CodePTMs to capture code structure. We conduct extensive experiments to integrate CAT-probing with representative CodePTMs for different programming languages. Experimental results show the effectiveness of CAT-probing in CodePTM interpretation. Our codes and data are publicly available at https://github.com/nchen909/CodeAttention.
summary: Code pre-trained models (CodePTMs) have recently demonstrated significant success in code intelligence. To interpret these models, some probing methods have been applied. However, these methods fail to consider the inherent characteristics of codes. In this paper, to address the problem, we propose a novel probing method CAT-probing to quantitatively interpret how CodePTMs attend code structure. We first denoise the input code sequences based on the token types pre-defined by the compilers to filter those tokens whose attention scores are too small. After that, we define a new metric CAT-score to measure the commonality between the token-level attention scores generated in CodePTMs and the pair-wise distances between corresponding AST nodes. The higher the CAT-score, the stronger the ability of CodePTMs to capture code structure. We conduct extensive experiments to integrate CAT-probing with representative CodePTMs for different programming languages. Experimental results show the effectiveness of CAT-probing in CodePTM interpretation. Our codes and data are publicly available at https://github.com/nchen909/CodeAttention.
abstract_zh: 代码预训练模型 (CodePTM) 最近在代码智能方面取得了巨大成功。为了解释这些模型，应用了一些探测方法。然而，这些方法没有考虑代码的固有特征。在本文中，为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新的探测方法 CAT-probing 来定量解释 CodePTM 如何参与代码结构。我们首先根据编译器预定义的标记类型对输入代码序列进行去噪，以过滤那些注意力分数太小的标记。之后，我们定义一个新的度量 CAT-score 来衡量 CodePTM 中生成的 token 级注意力分数与相应 AST 节点之间的成对距离之间的共性。 CAT 分数越高，CodePTM 捕获代码结构的能力越强。我们进行了大量的实验，将 CAT 探测与不同编程语言的代表性 CodePTM 集成。实验结果表明了 CAT 探测在 CodePTM 解释中的有效性。我们的代码和数据可在 https://github.com/nchen909/CodeAttention 公开获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出CAT-probing方法，通过定义CAT-score指标量化解释代码预训练模型如何关注代码结构。
AI.motivation: 现有代码模型解释方法未充分考虑代码的固有特性，缺乏有效衡量模型捕捉代码结构能力的指标。
AI.method: 基于编译器定义的标记类型过滤低注意力分数token，通过比较模型注意力得分与AST节点距离的共同性定义CAT-score指标。
AI.result: 实验验证了CAT-probing在多种编程语言CodePTMs中的有效性，代码和数据已开源。
AI.conclusion: CAT-probing为代码预训练模型的结构感知能力提供了可量化的解释方法，弥补了现有技术的不足。

search_text: CAT-probing: A Metric-based Approach to Interpret How Pre-trained Models for Programming Language Attend Code Structure CAT 探测：一种基于度量的方法来解释编程语言的预训练模型如何参与代码结构 Code pre-trained models (CodePTMs) have recently demonstrated significant success in code intelligence. To interpret these models, some probing methods have been applied. However, these methods fail to consider the inherent characteristics of codes. In this paper, to address the problem, we propose a novel probing method CAT-probing to quantitatively interpret how CodePTMs attend code structure. We first denoise the input code sequences based on the token types pre-defined by the compilers to filter those tokens whose attention scores are too small. After that, we define a new metric CAT-score to measure the commonality between the token-level attention scores generated in CodePTMs and the pair-wise distances between corresponding AST nodes. The higher the CAT-score, the stronger the ability of CodePTMs to capture code structure. We conduct extensive experiments to integrate CAT-probing with representative CodePTMs for different programming languages. Experimental results show the effectiveness of CAT-probing in CodePTM interpretation. Our codes and data are publicly available at https://github.com/nchen909/CodeAttention. 代码预训练模型 (CodePTM) 最近在代码智能方面取得了巨大成功。为了解释这些模型，应用了一些探测方法。然而，这些方法没有考虑代码的固有特征。在本文中，为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种新的探测方法 CAT-probing 来定量解释 CodePTM 如何参与代码结构。我们首先根据编译器预定义的标记类型对输入代码序列进行去噪，以过滤那些注意力分数太小的标记。之后，我们定义一个新的度量 CAT-score 来衡量 CodePTM 中生成的 token 级注意力分数与相应 AST 节点之间的成对距离之间的共性。 CAT 分数越高，CodePTM 捕获代码结构的能力越强。我们进行了大量的实验，将 CAT 探测与不同编程语言的代表性 CodePTM 集成。实验结果表明了 CAT 探测在 CodePTM 解释中的有效性。我们的代码和数据可在 https://github.com/nchen909/CodeAttention 公开获取。 本文提出CAT-probing方法，通过定义CAT-score指标量化解释代码预训练模型如何关注代码结构。 Nuo Chen,Qiushi Sun,Renyu Zhu,Xiang Li,Xuesong Lu,Ming Gao cs.SE,cs.AI,cs.LG,cs.PL

authors_text: Daniel Watson,William Chan,Ricardo Martin-Brualla,Jonathan Ho,Andrea Tagliasacchi,Mohammad Norouzi
title: Novel View Synthesis with Diffusion Models
title_zh: 使用扩散模型的新颖视图合成
abstract: We present 3DiM, a diffusion model for 3D novel view synthesis, which is able to translate a single input view into consistent and sharp completions across many views. The core component of 3DiM is a pose-conditional image-to-image diffusion model, which takes a source view and its pose as inputs, and generates a novel view for a target pose as output. 3DiM can generate multiple views that are 3D consistent using a novel technique called stochastic conditioning. The output views are generated autoregressively, and during the generation of each novel view, one selects a random conditioning view from the set of available views at each denoising step. We demonstrate that stochastic conditioning significantly improves the 3D consistency of a naive sampler for an image-to-image diffusion model, which involves conditioning on a single fixed view. We compare 3DiM to prior work on the SRN ShapeNet dataset, demonstrating that 3DiM's generated completions from a single view achieve much higher fidelity, while being approximately 3D consistent. We also introduce a new evaluation methodology, 3D consistency scoring, to measure the 3D consistency of a generated object by training a neural field on the model's output views. 3DiM is geometry free, does not rely on hyper-networks or test-time optimization for novel view synthesis, and allows a single model to easily scale to a large number of scenes.
summary: We present 3DiM, a diffusion model for 3D novel view synthesis, which is able to translate a single input view into consistent and sharp completions across many views. The core component of 3DiM is a pose-conditional image-to-image diffusion model, which takes a source view and its pose as inputs, and generates a novel view for a target pose as output. 3DiM can generate multiple views that are 3D consistent using a novel technique called stochastic conditioning. The output views are generated autoregressively, and during the generation of each novel view, one selects a random conditioning view from the set of available views at each denoising step. We demonstrate that stochastic conditioning significantly improves the 3D consistency of a naive sampler for an image-to-image diffusion model, which involves conditioning on a single fixed view. We compare 3DiM to prior work on the SRN ShapeNet dataset, demonstrating that 3DiM's generated completions from a single view achieve much higher fidelity, while being approximately 3D consistent. We also introduce a new evaluation methodology, 3D consistency scoring, to measure the 3D consistency of a generated object by training a neural field on the model's output views. 3DiM is geometry free, does not rely on hyper-networks or test-time optimization for novel view synthesis, and allows a single model to easily scale to a large number of scenes.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 3DiM，一种用于 3D 新颖视图合成的扩散模型，它能够将单个输入视图转换为跨多个视图的一致且清晰的完成效果。 3DiM 的核心组件是姿势条件图像到图像扩散模型，它将源视图及其姿势作为输入，并为目标姿势生成新颖的视图作为输出。 3DiM 可以使用称为随机调节的新技术生成 3D 一致的多个视图。输出视图是自回归生成的，并且在生成每个新视图的过程中，人们在每个去噪步骤中从一组可用视图中选择一个随机条件视图。我们证明，随机调节显着提高了图像到图像扩散模型的朴素采样器的 3D 一致性，其中涉及对单个固定视图的调节。我们将 3DiM 与 SRN ShapeNet 数据集上的先前工作进行比较，证明 3DiM 从单个视图生成的完成效果实现了更高的保真度，同时近似 3D 一致。我们还引入了一种新的评估方法，即 3D 一致性评分，通过在模型的输出视图上训练神经场来测量生成对象的 3D 一致性。 3DiM 不受几何影响，不依赖超网络或测试时优化来进行新颖的视图合成，并且允许单个模型轻松扩展到大量场景。

AI.tldr: 本文提出3DiM，一种基于扩散模型的3D新视角合成方法，能够从单视角输入生成多视角一致的高质量图像。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在3D一致性或生成质量上存在局限，且常依赖复杂结构或优化过程。本文旨在开发一种无需几何信息、无需超网络或测试时优化的高效3D视角合成方案。
AI.method: 设计姿态条件图像到图像的扩散模型，结合随机条件选择技术（stochastic conditioning），在去噪步骤中动态选择参考视角，通过自回归生成实现多视角一致性。
AI.result: 在SRN ShapeNet数据集上，3DiM生成结果在保真度和3D一致性上优于现有方法，并提出基于神经场的3D一致性评分新评估体系。
AI.conclusion: 3DiM通过扩散模型与随机条件策略实现了无需几何约束的高效3D视角合成，具有良好的可扩展性与实用性。

authors_text: Didier El Baz
title: On Parallel or Distributed Asynchronous Iterations with Unbounded Delays and Possible Out of Order Messages or Flexible Communication for Convex Optimization Problems and Machine Learning
title_zh: 关于凸优化问题和机器学习的具有无限延迟和可能的无序消息或灵活通信的并行或分布式异步迭代
comment: ef:2022 IEEE International Parallel and Distributed Processing Symposium Workshops (IPDPSW), May 2022, Lyon, France. pp.807-813
comments: ef:2022 IEEE International Parallel and Distributed Processing Symposium Workshops (IPDPSW), May 2022, Lyon, France. pp.807-813
abstract: We describe several features of parallel or distributed asynchronous iterative algorithms such as unbounded delays, possible out of order messages or flexible communication. We concentrate on the concept of macroiteration sequence which was introduced in order to study the convergence or termination of asynchronous iterations. A survey of asynchronous iterations for convex optimization problems is also presented. Finally, a new result of convergence for parallel or distributed asynchronous iterative algorithms with flexible communication for convex optimization problems and machine learning is proposed.
summary: We describe several features of parallel or distributed asynchronous iterative algorithms such as unbounded delays, possible out of order messages or flexible communication. We concentrate on the concept of macroiteration sequence which was introduced in order to study the convergence or termination of asynchronous iterations. A survey of asynchronous iterations for convex optimization problems is also presented. Finally, a new result of convergence for parallel or distributed asynchronous iterative algorithms with flexible communication for convex optimization problems and machine learning is proposed.
abstract_zh: 我们描述了并行或分布式异步迭代算法的几个特征，例如无限延迟、可能的无序消息或灵活的通信。我们集中讨论宏迭代序列的概念，引入它是为了研究异步迭代的收敛或终止。还介绍了凸优化问题的异步迭代的调查。最后，针对凸优化问题和机器学习，提出了一种具有灵活通信的并行或分布式异步迭代算法的收敛新结果。

AI.tldr: 本文研究了用于凸优化和机器学习的并行或分布式异步迭代算法，重点分析了无界延迟、消息乱序及灵活通信机制下的收敛性问题。
AI.motivation: 分布式计算中异步迭代面临通信延迟、消息乱序等挑战，传统同步方法效率受限，需设计适应性强的算法框架以保证收敛性。
AI.method: 通过引入宏观迭代序列概念，结合异步迭代理论与凸优化分析，提出适用于灵活通信模式的收敛性证明方法。
AI.result: 证明了在无界延迟和乱序消息场景下，基于灵活通信的异步迭代算法对凸优化问题的收敛性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的框架为设计高效分布式机器学习算法提供了理论支持，能够适应实际系统中的通信不确定性。

motivation: 分布式计算中异步迭代面临通信延迟、消息乱序等挑战，传统同步方法效率受限，需设计适应性强的算法框架以保证收敛性。
method: 通过引入宏观迭代序列概念，结合异步迭代理论与凸优化分析，提出适用于灵活通信模式的收敛性证明方法。
result: 证明了在无界延迟和乱序消息场景下，基于灵活通信的异步迭代算法对凸优化问题的收敛性。
conclusion: 提出的框架为设计高效分布式机器学习算法提供了理论支持，能够适应实际系统中的通信不确定性。
search_text: On Parallel or Distributed Asynchronous Iterations with Unbounded Delays and Possible Out of Order Messages or Flexible Communication for Convex Optimization Problems and Machine Learning 关于凸优化问题和机器学习的具有无限延迟和可能的无序消息或灵活通信的并行或分布式异步迭代 We describe several features of parallel or distributed asynchronous iterative algorithms such as unbounded delays, possible out of order messages or flexible communication. We concentrate on the concept of macroiteration sequence which was introduced in order to study the convergence or termination of asynchronous iterations. A survey of asynchronous iterations for convex optimization problems is also presented. Finally, a new result of convergence for parallel or distributed asynchronous iterative algorithms with flexible communication for convex optimization problems and machine learning is proposed. 我们描述了并行或分布式异步迭代算法的几个特征，例如无限延迟、可能的无序消息或灵活的通信。我们集中讨论宏迭代序列的概念，引入它是为了研究异步迭代的收敛或终止。还介绍了凸优化问题的异步迭代的调查。最后，针对凸优化问题和机器学习，提出了一种具有灵活通信的并行或分布式异步迭代算法的收敛新结果。 本文研究了用于凸优化和机器学习的并行或分布式异步迭代算法，重点分析了无界延迟、消息乱序及灵活通信机制下的收敛性问题。 Didier El Baz cs.DC,cs.LG,cs.NE

authors_text: Hanjiang Hu,Zuxin Liu,Linyi Li,Jiacheng Zhu,Ding Zhao
title: Robustness Certification of Visual Perception Models via Camera Motion Smoothing
title_zh: 通过相机运动平滑进行视觉感知模型的鲁棒性认证
comment: CoRL 2022 camera-ready version, 21 pages, 7 figures, 8 tables
comments: CoRL 2022 camera-ready version, 21 pages, 7 figures, 8 tables
abstract: A vast literature shows that the learning-based visual perception model is sensitive to adversarial noises, but few works consider the robustness of robotic perception models under widely-existing camera motion perturbations. To this end, we study the robustness of the visual perception model under camera motion perturbations to investigate the influence of camera motion on robotic perception. Specifically, we propose a motion smoothing technique for arbitrary image classification models, whose robustness under camera motion perturbations could be certified. The proposed robustness certification framework based on camera motion smoothing provides tight and scalable robustness guarantees for visual perception modules so that they are applicable to wide robotic applications. As far as we are aware, this is the first work to provide robustness certification for the deep perception module against camera motions, which improves the trustworthiness of robotic perception. A realistic indoor robotic dataset with a dense point cloud map for the entire room, MetaRoom, is introduced for the challenging certifiable robust perception task. We conduct extensive experiments to validate the certification approach via motion smoothing against camera motion perturbations. Our framework guarantees the certified accuracy of 81.7% against camera translation perturbation along depth direction within -0.1m ~ 0.1m. We also validate the effectiveness of our method on the real-world robot by conducting hardware experiments on the robotic arm with an eye-in-hand camera. The code is available at https://github.com/HanjiangHu/camera-motion-smoothing.
summary: A vast literature shows that the learning-based visual perception model is sensitive to adversarial noises, but few works consider the robustness of robotic perception models under widely-existing camera motion perturbations. To this end, we study the robustness of the visual perception model under camera motion perturbations to investigate the influence of camera motion on robotic perception. Specifically, we propose a motion smoothing technique for arbitrary image classification models, whose robustness under camera motion perturbations could be certified. The proposed robustness certification framework based on camera motion smoothing provides tight and scalable robustness guarantees for visual perception modules so that they are applicable to wide robotic applications. As far as we are aware, this is the first work to provide robustness certification for the deep perception module against camera motions, which improves the trustworthiness of robotic perception. A realistic indoor robotic dataset with a dense point cloud map for the entire room, MetaRoom, is introduced for the challenging certifiable robust perception task. We conduct extensive experiments to validate the certification approach via motion smoothing against camera motion perturbations. Our framework guarantees the certified accuracy of 81.7% against camera translation perturbation along depth direction within -0.1m ~ 0.1m. We also validate the effectiveness of our method on the real-world robot by conducting hardware experiments on the robotic arm with an eye-in-hand camera. The code is available at https://github.com/HanjiangHu/camera-motion-smoothing.
abstract_zh: 大量文献表明，基于学习的视觉感知模型对对抗性噪声敏感，但很少有研究考虑机器人感知模型在广泛存在的相机运动扰动下的鲁棒性。为此，我们研究了相机运动扰动下视觉感知模型的鲁棒性，以研究相机运动对机器人感知的影响。具体来说，我们提出了一种用于任意图像分类模型的运动平滑技术，其在相机运动扰动下的鲁棒性可以得到认证。所提出的基于相机运动平滑的鲁棒性认证框架为视觉感知模块提供了严格且可扩展的鲁棒性保证，使其适用于广泛的机器人应用。据我们所知，这是第一个为深度感知模块针对相机运动提供鲁棒性认证的工作，从而提高了机器人感知的可信度。引入了一个真实的室内机器人数据集，该数据集具有整个房间的密集点云图 MetaRoom，用于具有挑战性的可认证鲁棒感知任务。我们进行了大量的实验，通过针对相机运动扰动的运动平滑来验证认证方法。我们的框架保证-0.1m ~ 0.1m 范围内沿深度方向的相机平移扰动的认证精度为 81.7%。我们还通过在带有手眼相机的机械臂上进行硬件实验来验证我们的方法在现实机器人上的有效性。代码可在 https://github.com/Han JiangHu/camera-motion-smoothing 获取。

AI.tldr: 研究视觉感知模型在相机运动扰动下的鲁棒性，并提出基于相机运动平滑的鲁棒性认证框架。
AI.motivation: 现有研究忽视了相机运动扰动对机器人感知模型的影响，而实际应用中此类扰动普遍存在，需提升模型鲁棒性以增强可信度。
AI.method: 提出运动平滑技术及鲁棒性认证框架，通过平滑相机运动扰动为视觉感知模块提供可扩展的鲁棒性保证。
AI.result: 框架在-0.1m至0.1m深度方向平移扰动下认证准确率达81.7%，并在真实机械臂上验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 首次为深度感知模块提供相机运动鲁棒性认证，提升机器人感知的可信度，并引入MetaRoom数据集推动相关研究。

authors_text: Gabriel Silva,Paulo Knob,Rubens Montanha,Soraia Musse
title: WebCrowds: An Authoring Tool for Crowd Simulation
title_zh: WebCrowds：人群模拟的创作工具
comment: Accepted on the 2022 Brazilian Symposium on Games and Digital Entertainment (SBGames 2022 - https://sbgames.org/sbgames2022/). 7 pages, 4 figures, conference
comments: Accepted on the 2022 Brazilian Symposium on Games and Digital Entertainment (SBGames 2022 - https://sbgames.org/sbgames2022/). 7 pages, 4 figures, conference
abstract: Crowd simulation is an area of research largely used in the game industry. From the movement of a single NPC to the movement of an entire army, crowd simulation methods can be used to move agents through the environment while avoiding collisions with obstacles and between each other. Thus, it is important that game developers have access to crowd simulation tools that are both powerful and easy to use. In this paper, we present WebCrowds, an authoring tool for crowd simulation which can be used by anyone to build environments and simulate the movement of agents. The results achieved by our research suggest that WebCrowds is easy to use, delivers trustworthy simulation results, and can be used as an authoring tool for game developers who need to simulate crowds in their games.
summary: Crowd simulation is an area of research largely used in the game industry. From the movement of a single NPC to the movement of an entire army, crowd simulation methods can be used to move agents through the environment while avoiding collisions with obstacles and between each other. Thus, it is important that game developers have access to crowd simulation tools that are both powerful and easy to use. In this paper, we present WebCrowds, an authoring tool for crowd simulation which can be used by anyone to build environments and simulate the movement of agents. The results achieved by our research suggest that WebCrowds is easy to use, delivers trustworthy simulation results, and can be used as an authoring tool for game developers who need to simulate crowds in their games.
abstract_zh: 人群模拟是游戏行业广泛使用的一个研究领域。从单个 NPC 的移动到整个军队的移动，群体模拟方法可用于在环境中移动代理，同时避免与障碍物以及彼此之间的碰撞。因此，游戏开发人员能够使用功能强大且易于使用的人群模拟工具非常重要。在本文中，我们介绍了 WebCrowds，这是一种用于人群模拟的创作工具，任何人都可以使用它来构建环境并模拟代理的运动。我们的研究结果表明，WebCrowds 易于使用，可提供值得信赖的模拟结果，并且可以用作需要在游戏中模拟人群的游戏开发人员的创作工具。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一种名为WebCrowds的群体模拟作者工具，用于创建环境并模拟代理的移动。
AI.motivation: 游戏开发者需要强大且易用的工具来模拟复杂场景中的群体行为，如NPC移动和碰撞避免。
AI.method: 开发了WebCrowds工具，提供直观界面供用户构建环境并模拟代理移动，结合碰撞检测与路径规划算法。
AI.result: 实验表明WebCrowds易于使用，能生成可靠的模拟结果，满足游戏开发中群体模拟需求。
AI.conclusion: WebCrowds为游戏开发者提供了一个高效、用户友好的群体模拟解决方案，可有效支持复杂场景的开发。

authors_text: Jean Ogier du Terrail,Samy-Safwan Ayed,Edwige Cyffers,Felix Grimberg,Chaoyang He,Regis Loeb,Paul Mangold,Tanguy Marchand,Othmane Marfoq,Erum Mushtaq,Boris Muzellec,Constantin Philippenko,Santiago Silva,Maria Teleńczuk,Shadi Albarqouni,Salman Avestimehr,Aurélien Bellet,Aymeric Dieuleveut,Martin Jaggi,Sai Praneeth Karimireddy,Marco Lorenzi,Giovanni Neglia,Marc Tommasi,Mathieu Andreux
title: FLamby: Datasets and Benchmarks for Cross-Silo Federated Learning in Realistic Healthcare Settings
title_zh: FLamby：现实医疗保健环境中跨孤岛联邦学习的数据集和基准
comment: Accepted to NeurIPS, Datasets and Benchmarks Track, this version fixes typos in the datasets' table and the appendix
comments: Accepted to NeurIPS, Datasets and Benchmarks Track, this version fixes typos in the datasets' table and the appendix
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is a novel approach enabling several clients holding sensitive data to collaboratively train machine learning models, without centralizing data. The cross-silo FL setting corresponds to the case of few ($2$--$50$) reliable clients, each holding medium to large datasets, and is typically found in applications such as healthcare, finance, or industry. While previous works have proposed representative datasets for cross-device FL, few realistic healthcare cross-silo FL datasets exist, thereby slowing algorithmic research in this critical application. In this work, we propose a novel cross-silo dataset suite focused on healthcare, FLamby (Federated Learning AMple Benchmark of Your cross-silo strategies), to bridge the gap between theory and practice of cross-silo FL. FLamby encompasses 7 healthcare datasets with natural splits, covering multiple tasks, modalities, and data volumes, each accompanied with baseline training code. As an illustration, we additionally benchmark standard FL algorithms on all datasets. Our flexible and modular suite allows researchers to easily download datasets, reproduce results and re-use the different components for their research. FLamby is available at~\url{www.github.com/owkin/flamby}.

FLamby encompasses 7 healthcare datasets with natural splits, covering multiple tasks, modalities, and data volumes, each accompanied with baseline training code. As an illustration, we additionally benchmark standard FL algorithms on all datasets. Our flexible and modular suite allows researchers to easily download datasets, reproduce results and re-use the different components for their research. FLamby is available at~\url{www.github.com/owkin/flamby}.
abstract_zh: 联合学习 (FL) 是一种新颖的方法，使持有敏感数据的多个客户端能够协作训练机器学习模型，而无需集中数据。跨筒仓 FL 设置对应于少数（2 美元 - 50 美元）可靠客户端的情况，每个客户端都持有中型到大型数据集，通常出现在医疗保健、金融或工业等应用中。虽然之前的工作提出了跨设备 FL 的代表性数据集，但现实的医疗保健跨筒仓 FL 数据集很少存在，从而减慢了这一关键应用中的算法研究。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个专注于医疗保健的新型跨孤岛数据集套件 FLamby（跨孤岛策略的联邦学习 AMple 基准），以弥合跨孤岛 FL 理论与实践之间的差距。 Flamby 包含 7 个具有自然分割的医疗数据集，涵盖多个任务、模式和数据量，每个数据集都附带基线训练代码。作为说明，我们还在所有数据集上对标准 FL 算法进行了基准测试。我们灵活的模块化套件使研究人员能够轻松下载数据集、重现结果并重复使用不同的组件进行研究。 Flamby 可以在 ~\url{www.github.com/owkin/flamby} 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出FLamby，一个面向医疗健康领域的跨机构联邦学习数据集和基准测试套件，旨在弥补理论与实践之间的差距。
AI.motivation: 现有研究缺乏真实医疗场景下的跨机构联邦学习数据集，限制了该领域算法的研究进展。
AI.method: 构建包含7个医疗数据集的FLamby套件，涵盖多种任务、模态和数据规模，并提供基准训练代码及标准联邦学习算法的测试结果。
AI.result: FLamby支持研究人员下载数据集、复现结果并复用组件，已开源至GitHub平台。
AI.conclusion: FLamby为跨机构联邦学习在医疗领域的应用提供了标准化基准，推动了算法研究与实践的结合。

search_text: FLamby: Datasets and Benchmarks for Cross-Silo Federated Learning in Realistic Healthcare Settings FLamby：现实医疗保健环境中跨孤岛联邦学习的数据集和基准 Federated Learning (FL) is a novel approach enabling several clients holding sensitive data to collaboratively train machine learning models, without centralizing data. The cross-silo FL setting corresponds to the case of few ($2$--$50$) reliable clients, each holding medium to large datasets, and is typically found in applications such as healthcare, finance, or industry. While previous works have proposed representative datasets for cross-device FL, few realistic healthcare cross-silo FL datasets exist, thereby slowing algorithmic research in this critical application. In this work, we propose a novel cross-silo dataset suite focused on healthcare, FLamby (Federated Learning AMple Benchmark of Your cross-silo strategies), to bridge the gap between theory and practice of cross-silo FL. FLamby encompasses 7 healthcare datasets with natural splits, covering multiple tasks, modalities, and data volumes, each accompanied with baseline training code. As an illustration, we additionally benchmark standard FL algorithms on all datasets. Our flexible and modular suite allows researchers to easily download datasets, reproduce results and re-use the different components for their research. FLamby is available at~\url{www.github.com/owkin/flamby}. 联合学习 (FL) 是一种新颖的方法，使持有敏感数据的多个客户端能够协作训练机器学习模型，而无需集中数据。跨筒仓 FL 设置对应于少数（2 美元 - 50 美元）可靠客户端的情况，每个客户端都持有中型到大型数据集，通常出现在医疗保健、金融或工业等应用中。虽然之前的工作提出了跨设备 FL 的代表性数据集，但现实的医疗保健跨筒仓 FL 数据集很少存在，从而减慢了这一关键应用中的算法研究。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个专注于医疗保健的新型跨孤岛数据集套件 FLamby（跨孤岛策略的联邦学习 AMple 基准），以弥合跨孤岛 FL 理论与实践之间的差距。 Flamby 包含 7 个具有自然分割的医疗数据集，涵盖多个任务、模式和数据量，每个数据集都附带基线训练代码。作为说明，我们还在所有数据集上对标准 FL 算法进行了基准测试。我们灵活的模块化套件使研究人员能够轻松下载数据集、重现结果并重复使用不同的组件进行研究。 Flamby 可以在 ~\url{www.github.com/owkin/flamby} 上找到。 本文提出FLamby，一个面向医疗健康领域的跨机构联邦学习数据集和基准测试套件，旨在弥补理论与实践之间的差距。 Jean Ogier du Terrail,Samy-Safwan Ayed,Edwige Cyffers,Felix Grimberg,Chaoyang He,Regis Loeb,Paul Mangold,Tanguy Marchand,Othmane Marfoq,Erum Mushtaq,Boris Muzellec,Constantin Philippenko,Santiago Silva,Maria Teleńczuk,Shadi Albarqouni,Salman Avestimehr,Aurélien Bellet,Aymeric Dieuleveut,Martin Jaggi,Sai Praneeth Karimireddy,Marco Lorenzi,Giovanni Neglia,Marc Tommasi,Mathieu Andreux cs.LG,cs.CV

authors_text: Tien-Phat Nguyen,Trong-Thang Pham,Tri Nguyen,Hieu Le,Dung Nguyen,Hau Lam,Phong Nguyen,Jennifer Fowler,Minh-Triet Tran,Ngan Le
title: EmbryosFormer: Deformable Transformer and Collaborative Encoding-Decoding for Embryos Stage Development Classification
title_zh: EmbryosFormer：用于胚胎阶段发育分类的可变形变压器和协作编码解码
abstract: The timing of cell divisions in early embryos during the In-Vitro Fertilization (IVF) process is a key predictor of embryo viability. However, observing cell divisions in Time-Lapse Monitoring (TLM) is a time-consuming process and highly depends on experts. In this paper, we propose EmbryosFormer, a computational model to automatically detect and classify cell divisions from original time-lapse images. Our proposed network is designed as an encoder-decoder deformable transformer with collaborative heads. The transformer contracting path predicts per-image labels and is optimized by a classification head. The transformer expanding path models the temporal coherency between embryo images to ensure monotonic non-decreasing constraint and is optimized by a segmentation head. Both contracting and expanding paths are synergetically learned by a collaboration head. We have benchmarked our proposed EmbryosFormer on two datasets: a public dataset with mouse embryos with 8-cell stage and an in-house dataset with human embryos with 4-cell stage. Source code: https://github.com/UARK-AICV/Embryos.
summary: The timing of cell divisions in early embryos during the In-Vitro Fertilization (IVF) process is a key predictor of embryo viability. However, observing cell divisions in Time-Lapse Monitoring (TLM) is a time-consuming process and highly depends on experts. In this paper, we propose EmbryosFormer, a computational model to automatically detect and classify cell divisions from original time-lapse images. Our proposed network is designed as an encoder-decoder deformable transformer with collaborative heads. The transformer contracting path predicts per-image labels and is optimized by a classification head. The transformer expanding path models the temporal coherency between embryo images to ensure monotonic non-decreasing constraint and is optimized by a segmentation head. Both contracting and expanding paths are synergetically learned by a collaboration head. We have benchmarked our proposed EmbryosFormer on two datasets: a public dataset with mouse embryos with 8-cell stage and an in-house dataset with human embryos with 4-cell stage. Source code: https://github.com/UARK-AICV/Embryos.
abstract_zh: 体外受精 (IVF) 过程中早期胚胎细胞分裂的时间是胚胎活力的关键预测因素。然而，在延时监测（TLM）中观察细胞分裂是一个耗时的过程，并且高度依赖于专家。在本文中，我们提出了 EmbryosFormer，一种从原始延时图像中自动检测和分类细胞分裂的计算模型。我们提出的网络被设计为具有协作头的编码器-解码器可变形变压器。变压器收缩路径预测每个图像的标签，并通过分类头进行优化。变压器扩展路径对胚胎图像之间的时间一致性进行建模，以确保单调非递减约束，并通过分割头进行优化。协作负责人协同学习收缩和扩展路径。我们在两个数据集上对我们提出的 EmbryosFormer 进行了基准测试：一个包含 8 细胞阶段小鼠胚胎的公共数据集和一个包含 4 细胞阶段人类胚胎的内部数据集。源代码：https://github.com/UARK-AICV/Embryos。

AI.tldr: 本文提出EmbryosFormer模型，用于自动检测和分类体外受精胚胎的发育阶段。
AI.motivation: 胚胎细胞分裂时间是评估胚胎存活率的关键指标，但传统时间延迟监测依赖专家手动观察，耗时且效率低。
AI.method: 设计基于可变形Transformer的编码器-解码器架构，包含分类头（处理图像标签）、分割头（建模时间一致性）及协作头（协同学习两个路径）。
AI.result: 在小鼠8细胞阶段和人类4细胞阶段数据集上验证，模型有效实现了胚胎发育阶段的自动分类。
AI.conclusion: EmbryosFormer通过时序建模和协作学习机制，提升了胚胎发育分类的自动化水平和准确性。
